REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY INJUNCTION SHOULD NOT ISSUE IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN AND SHOW LIKELIHOOD OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS AND IRREPARABLE HARM BY COMPETENT ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. THE COURT PROVIDED THE MOVING PARTY WITH MULTIPLE OPPORTUNITIES TO PROVIDE SUCH EVIDENCE. SEE APRIL 3, 2020 AND APRIL 9, 2020 ORDERS. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: A. PASCUAL, NOT REPORTED. (501)

Order To Show Cause Why Injunction Should Not Issue;
Off Calendar Apr-30-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

STEPHEN SUSTRICK (650-823-6173) PLAINTIFF VIA COURTCALL.
NO OTHER APPEARANCES.

The Court inquires if the plaintiff complied with the tentative ruling rule and finds the plaintiff is not in compliance. The court hears no argument from plaintff. The plaintiff argues regarding an affidavit and the Court instructs regarding admissible evidence. The Court further reads from plaintiff declaration indicating the plaintiff did not provide competent and admissible evidence.

THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:

Order To Show Cause Why Injunction Should Not Issue IS DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain its burden and show likelihood of prevailing on the merits and irreparable harm by competent admissible evidence. The Court provided the moving party with multiple opportunities to provide such evidence. See April 3, 2020 and April 9, 2020 Orders. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: A. PASCUAL, NOT REPORTED. (501)
